title: Gevo Awarded Patent for Ethanol-to-Olefins Process to Produce Renewable Jet Fuel
content: Gevo, Inc. has been awarded U.S. Patent No. 12,486,207 B2, securing broader protections for various catalyst combinations and explicitly covering its ethanol-to-olefins (ETO) technology for fuel production. This patented process converts ethanol into light olefins, which can then be transformed into transportation fuels through established Alcohol-to-Jet (ATJ) methods, enabling the production of renewable jet fuel and cost-effective chemical building blocks like bioâ€‘propylene. Developed in collaboration with LG Chem for chemicals and with Axens and IFP Energies nouvelles for fuel applications, the nextâ€‘generation ETO technology promises up to 35 % lower capital and operating costs than existing methods. Dr. Paul Bloom, Gevoâ€™s president, emphasized that the patent strengthens their proprietary position and advances more cost-efficient renewable jet fuel pathways. Gevo plans to pioneer ETO deployment in North America, supporting economic growth and rural jobs while Axens provides global licensing, equipment, and engineering services.
author: Analysts Account
